title: News and updates | Employment New Zealand
content:
## News and updates Ngā rongo kōrero me ngā whakahou
News and media releases from Employment New Zealand.
- 15 Sept 2022 Most COVID-19 rules have now ended New Zealand’s COVID-19 Protection Framework (traffic lights) ended at 11.59pm on Monday 12 September.
New Zealand’s COVID-19 Protection Framework (traffic lights) ended at 11.59pm on Monday 12 September.
- 15 Sept 2022 Public holiday to mark passing of Queen Elizabeth II To mark the passing of Queen Elizabeth II, the Government has announced a one-off national public holiday, to take place on Monday 26 September 2022. Public holidays
To mark the passing of Queen Elizabeth II, the Government has announced a one-off national public holiday, to take place on Monday 26 September 2022.
- 12 Sept 2022 Employment Court finds employer was not entitled to require lockdown leave, due to process errors The Employment Court has ruled that Carter Holt Harvey LVL did not consult adequately with its employees before requiring them to take annual leave during the nationwide Level 4 lockdown in 2020. Legal case
The Employment Court has ruled that Carter Holt Harvey LVL did not consult adequately with its employees before requiring them to take annual leave during the nationwide Level 4 lockdown in 2020.
- 7 Jul 2022 Labour Inspectorate enforcement yields $313k Enforcement action has recovered wage arrears and penalties in excess of $313,000 from the owner of Dunedin-based Romeeco Bakery. Legal case
Enforcement action has recovered wage arrears and penalties in excess of $313,000 from the owner of Dunedin-based Romeeco Bakery.
- 30 Jun 2022 Changes to COVID-19 reinfection advice The Ministry of Health has updated its advice around possible reinfection with COVID-19 within 90 days of a previous infection, in light of evidence that reinfections can occur earlier than 90 days.
The Ministry of Health has updated its advice around possible reinfection with COVID-19 within 90 days of a previous infection, in light of evidence that reinfections can occur earlier than 90 days.
- 15 Jun 2022 Parental leave payments to increase Parental leave payments will increase by 6.33% from Friday, 1 July 2022, to match the rise in the average weekly earnings. Parental leave
Parental leave payments will increase by 6.33% from Friday, 1 July 2022, to match the rise in the average weekly earnings.
- 8 Jun 2022 Takeaway delivery driver an employee, ERA finds A driver delivering takeaway food via an online app was an employee of the company providing the platform, and not an independent contractor, the Employment Relations Authority has found. Legal case
A driver delivering takeaway food via an online app was an employee of the company providing the platform, and not an independent contractor, the Employment Relations Authority has found.
- 8 Jun 2022 New Zealand celebrates first Matariki public holiday The Matariki public holiday – the first public holiday to recognise Te Ao Māori – will be on Friday 24 June 2022. Public holidays
The Matariki public holiday – the first public holiday to recognise Te Ao Māori – will be on Friday 24 June 2022.
